title: Enhanced Printing
text: Enhanced Printing Enhanced printing uses native Windows features to print from Uniface applications on Windows. This makes it possible to print using proportional fonts, images, and layouts that better match what is displayed on the screen (widget-dependent printing). The Uniface widgets are printed in the proportional font specified for that widget. If they have a ValRep defined, the representation is printed instead of the value if this makes sense. Additionally, operating system dialogs are used during the print process, allowing the user to modify the print behavior or even cancel printing. Uniface provides several enhanced printing device translation tables, including the P_MSWINX for printing on Microsoft Windows and the P_X11 for generating PostScript files. PostScript files can be directed to a third-party utility for printing or conversion to PDF. This is useful for batch PDF generation, but also provides enhanced printing capabilities on non-Windows platforms. Fonts for Enhanced Printing In the same manner that logical fonts are used when a form is displayed, logical fonts are also used when a report is printed. The mechanism for defining logical fonts is environment-specific. Different physical fonts can be used depending on whether the data will be printed or displayed. For example, a label could be painted with the logical font lab_font . The physical to logical font mapping could define this font as Arial Bold 10 pt for display, but Arial Bold 12 pt for printing. If you are using an enhanced printer driver, consider how data handled by widgets is reported. Table 1. Enhanced Printing for Selected Widgets Widget Description Check box One-character box containing one of: X (on), empty (off), or shaded (null) . Command button The glyph or the text. Vertical expansion does not take place with a command button. Drop-down list The representation of the current selection. Edit box The text in the edit box. List box The representation of the current selection or selections. Picture The picture. Radio group The representation of the current selection. Spin button The text. Map Shows the picture and the polygon when a selection is active. Unifield The text, as formatted by the text editor, using a non-proportional font. Related Links Device Translation Tables for Printing Printer device tables are a collection of sequences (control codes) associated with specific logical functions (such as bold, form feed, and init). The sequences begin with the escape character and are referred to as escape sequences. PostScript Support Uniface supports output to PostScript files. PostScript files can be directed to a third-party utility for printing or conversion to PDF. This is useful for batch PDF generation, but also provides enhanced printing capabilities on non-Windows platforms. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
